title: Amazing Fantasy #15 + Amazing Spider-Man #1-33 (Stan Lee & Steve Ditko Run)
blurb: The definitive origin. Start here. Peter becomes Spider-Man, meets his first rogues gallery, and learns the ropes of being a hero. The foundation of everything that follows.

title: Amazing Spider-Man #39-122 (Stan Lee & John Romita Sr. Run)
blurb: The Romita era. Essential storytelling. Eventually bleeds into the Gerry Conway Run.

title: Amazing Spider-Man #252-300 (Black Suit & Venom Run)
blurb: The alien symbiote saga through to Venom's full debut in #300. (Technically you need to read Secret Wars #8 to get the full story)

title: Kraven's Last Hunt (ASM #293-294, Spectacular #131-132, Web #31-32)
blurb: Widely considered the best Spider-Man story ever written. A dark, psychological tale that explores the depths of Peter Parker's character. A must-read for any Spider-Man fan.

title: Amazing Spider-Man by J. Michael Straczynski (Vol. 2, #30-58)
blurb: The best modern Spider-Man run. Reinvents the character without betraying him. (Isaiah's Personal Favorite)

title: Ultimate Spider-Man #1-160 (Brian Michael Bendis)
blurb: A complete, self-contained retelling of Spider-Man for modern readers. The ultimate starting point for new readers

title: Superior Spider-Man #1-31 (Dan Slott)
blurb: What if Doctor Octopus was Spider-Man? An interesting take on our Friendly Neighborhood Web-Slinger. Read after ASM #700.
